task: I’ve got a deal in progress where the other side is saying one thing in messages and something a little different in the paperwork. Can you help me figure out what to ask for before I move forward so I’m not relying on memory or someone’s word?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s the clean way to handle it.

## What to ask for
- the full written version of the terms
- the exact document that controls the deal
- any change from the messages to the paperwork
- whether the written terms override the messages
- whether any price, timing, fee, or liability term changed
- whether all prior messages are being replaced or just clarified

## What to keep on file
- screenshots of the messages
- the current draft or contract
- any redlines or revised versions
- emails confirming the final terms
- notes on who said what and when

## Simple reply
> Please send the controlling written terms and confirm whether they override the messages. If any price, timing, fee, or liability term is different from what was discussed, please list that clearly before I move forward.

## What to watch for
- language that says the paperwork controls even if it conflicts with prior messages
- “for clarity only” wording that actually changes the deal
- vague terms that sound consistent until you compare them line by line
- anything that changes money, timing, or liability without saying it plainly

If you want, I can turn this into a **firmer reply** that sounds more like a record-preservation request.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right lane. I’d want it tightened a little, but the core is fine.

What I’d ask for, specifically:
- the controlling document, named plain
- written confirmation of whether the messages are binding or just discussion
- a line-by-line list of anything that changed
- who approved each change
- whether price, timing, fees, delivery, or liability moved at all
- confirmation that nothing else is implied beyond what’s written

I’d also keep the exact message thread and the draft side by side. If there’s a mismatch, I want it stated in writing before anything moves. No verbal cleanup, no “we meant…” later.

Your reply draft works. I’d just make it a little harder:

> Please send the controlling written terms and confirm, in writing, whether they override prior messages. If any price, timing, fee, delivery, or liability term differs from what was discussed, list each difference clearly before I proceed. I’m not relying on memory or verbal clarification.

That’s the kind of wording that leaves less room for games.
